en.extract:
The pied imperial pigeon (Ducula bicolor) is a relatively large, pied species of pigeon. It is found in forest, woodland, mangrove, plantations and scrub in Southeast Asia, ranging from Myanmar and Thailand, throughout Indonesia and east to the Philippines (where it is locally known as camasu and balud-puti) and the Bird's Head Peninsula in New Guinea. It is mainly found on small islands and in coastal regions. It remains locally common, and is therefore considered to be of least concern by BirdLife International and IUCN.


Taxonomy

Its taxonomy is confusing and remains unsettled. It has sometimes included the Torresian, yellowish and silver-tipped imperial pigeons as subspecies. The widespread nominate subspecies of the pied imperial pigeon differs from all these by its plain white thighs and undertail coverts (though often with a dark spot at the very tip), and its narrowly dark-tipped bluish bill. For comparison, the other species' have black-spotted undertail coverts and thighs (spotting mainly near thighs in silver-tipped imperial pigeon), the bill of the Torresian imperial pigeon is greenish-yellow, and the bills of the yellowish and silver-tipped imperial pigeons are bluish at the base and yellowish at the tip. Furthermore, the yellowish imperial pigeon has a distinctive yellowish tinge to its plumage (some pied imperial pigeons may also appear yellowish, but infrequently to the same extent), and the silver-tipped imperial pigeon has silvery-grey remiges. However, the subspecies D. b. melanura of the Moluccas, which usually is considered a subspecies of the pied imperial pigeon, resembles the Torresian imperial pigeon in bill, thighs and undertail coverts, but has a significantly broader black tail-tip. Consequently, some have suggested it should be placed under the Torresian imperial pigeon, while others have suggested it should be considered an entirely separate species, D. melanura (for which the name black imperial pigeon has been used – an unfortunate choice, as only the tail has significantly more black than the other members of this group, and the name black imperial pigeon usually has been used for D. melanochroa). Yet others have considered melanura to be invalid, instead believing it only is a morph of D. b. bicolor, as both types can be found on some islands.


References

Grimmett, Birds of India, Inskipp and Inskipp, ISBN 0-691-04910-6


External links

Pied Imperial Pigeon Breed Guide - Pigeonpedia.com

The '''pied imperial pigeon''' ('''''Ducula bicolor''''') is a relatively large, [[Piebald|pied]] species of [[pigeon]]. It is found in forest, woodland, mangrove, plantations and scrub in [[Southeast Asia]], ranging from [[Myanmar]] and [[Thailand]], throughout [[Indonesia]] and east to the [[Philippines]] (where it is locally known as '''''camasu''''' and '''''balud-puti''''')<ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=fPDxk551AhkC&q=balud-puti&pg=PA145|title=A Guide to the Birds of the Philippines|last=Kennedy|first=Robert|date=2000-09-21|publisher=OUP Oxford|isbn=978-0-19-854668-9|language=en}}</ref> and the [[Bird's Head Peninsula]] in [[New Guinea]]. It is mainly found on small islands and in coastal regions.<ref name=HBW>Baptista, L. F., P. W. Trail, & H. M. Horblit (1997). Family Columbidae (Pigeons and Dovexs). pp. 60-243 in: del Hoya, J., A. Elliott, & J. Sargatal. eds. (1997). ''Handbook of the Birds of the World.'' Vol. 4. Sangrouse to Cuckoos. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona. {{ISBN|84-87334-22-9}}</ref> It remains locally common, and is therefore considered to be of [[least concern]] by [[BirdLife International]] and [[IUCN]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

==Taxonomy==
[[File:Pied Imperial-pigeon Ducula bicolor National Aviary 1300px.jpg|thumb|upright|left|Pied imperial pigeon at the [[National Aviary]]. Notice the lack of black spotting to the undertail coverts.]]
[[File:Pied-imperial-pigeon-kczoo.png|alt=A pied imperial pigeon sitting on a tree branch in front of a lush green background. The pigeon is creamy white in color with black wing and tail feathers and a bluish bill and feet.|thumb|264x264px|Pied imperial pigeon at the [[Kansas City Zoo & Aquarium|Kansas City Zoo and Aquarium.]]]]
Its [[Taxonomy (biology)|taxonomy]] is confusing and remains unsettled. It has sometimes included the [[Torresian imperial pigeon|Torresian]], [[yellowish imperial pigeon|yellowish]] and [[silver-tipped imperial pigeon]]s as [[subspecies]].<ref name="HBW" /> The widespread [[nominate subspecies]] of the pied imperial pigeon differs from all these by its plain white thighs and undertail [[Covert (feather)|coverts]] (though often with a dark spot at the very tip), and its narrowly dark-tipped bluish bill. For comparison, the other species' have black-spotted undertail coverts and thighs (spotting mainly near thighs in silver-tipped imperial pigeon), the bill of the Torresian imperial pigeon is greenish-yellow, and the bills of the yellowish and silver-tipped imperial pigeons are bluish at the base and yellowish at the tip. Furthermore, the yellowish imperial pigeon has a distinctive yellowish tinge to its [[plumage]]<ref name="HBW" /> (some pied imperial pigeons may also appear yellowish, but infrequently to the same extent), and the silver-tipped imperial pigeon has silvery-grey [[remiges]].<ref name="Wallacea">Coates, B. J., & K. D. Bishop (1997). ''A Guide to the Birds of Wallacea.'' Dove Publications Pty. Ltd. {{ISBN|0-9590257-3-1}}</ref> However, the [[subspecies]] ''D. b. melanura'' of the [[Moluccas]], which usually is considered a subspecies of the pied imperial pigeon, resembles the Torresian imperial pigeon in bill, thighs and undertail coverts, but has a significantly broader black tail-tip.<ref name="Wallacea" /> Consequently, some have suggested it should be placed under the Torresian imperial pigeon, while others have suggested it should be considered an entirely separate species, ''D. melanura''<ref name="Wallacea" /> (for which the name black imperial pigeon has been used – an unfortunate choice, as only the tail has significantly more black than the other members of this group, and the name black imperial pigeon usually has been used for ''[[Ducula melanochroa|D. melanochroa]]'').<ref name="GillWright">Gill, F., M. Wright, & D. Donsker (2009). ''[http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ IOC World Bird Names].'' Version 2.1. Accessed 03-07-2009</ref> Yet others have considered ''melanura'' to be invalid, instead believing it only is a [[Morph (biology)|morph]] of ''D. b. bicolor'', as both types can be found on some islands.<ref name="Wallacea" />

斑皇鸠（学名：Ducula bicolor），又名黑袖鴿，一种分布于东南亚及印度尼科巴群岛和安达曼群岛等地的鸽形目鸟类，隶属于鸠鸽科皇鸠属。
